opportunity.title: Show HN: An open-source safety net for home hemodialysis
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The complexity of error handling in real-time safety systems is often underestimated. Routing LLM responses based on confidence scores alone isn’t enough; you’ll need a layered approach with deterministic checks—think constraint satisfaction—to validate outputs before triggering actions, especially when dealing with potentially life-threatening scenarios. Consider the trade-offs between latency and safety when designing these validation layers.
actions.reasoning: My experience building AI agents that interact with external systems and manage complex workflows directly applies to the challenges of creating a reliable safety net for a life-critical process like home hemodialysis.
